result:
Fixed — the dot wave now lives only in the bottom band of the page, and everything above it is back to exactly how level-3 looked.

What changed: instead of a `fixed` full-viewport layer behind the whole site, `#dotted-surface` is now `position: absolute` anchored to the foot of the document, about 46vh tall (clamped 360–620px). The footer is the one section with a transparent background, so the wave shows through it and nothing else. That let me revert the compromises I'd made last round — the hero, the sections, and the page background are back to their level-3 opaque values, and the `--veil` tokens are gone.

Two adjustments the new framing required: the camera now tilts down onto the wave (`lookAt`), because in a wide-but-short band the original straight-ahead camera put the horizon above the crop and you'd just see undifferentiated dots; and the top edge of the band gets a gradient mask so the field dissolves into the page rather than starting at a hard seam. The renderer sizes itself to the band via a `ResizeObserver` rather than to the window.

I verified in the browser at 1440×900: the full-page screenshot shows a clean hero and mid-page in light theme with dots confined to the footer, dark theme shows the wave clearly with a visible horizon at the top of the footer, and there are no console errors. It's still always on with no trigger, still re-themes with the toggle, and level-3 is untouched (`422df4c5…`, unchanged).
